message:
feat(fr-persist): decode upstream RDB compact-encoding type tags 11/16/17/18/20 (br-frankenredis-aqgx)

Adds the missing decoders so a `dump.rdb` produced by upstream redis-server
loads cleanly through `fr_persist::decode_rdb`, regardless of which
listpack / intset / quicklist threshold the seeded structures fall under.

* `RDB_TYPE_SET_INTSET` (11) — string-wrapped intset blob, decoded into
  `RdbValue::Set(Vec<Vec<u8>>)` via a new `decode_intset_members` helper
  (16/32/64-bit element widths, sorted decimal-string canonicalisation).
* `RDB_TYPE_SET_LISTPACK` (20) — string-wrapped listpack of members.
* `RDB_TYPE_HASH_LISTPACK` (16) — listpack of (field, value) pairs;
  rejects odd-length entry vectors as malformed.
* `RDB_TYPE_ZSET_LISTPACK` (17) — listpack of (member, decimal-score)
  pairs; rejects non-numeric scores as malformed.
* `RDB_TYPE_LIST_QUICKLIST_2` (18) — N nodes of (container, blob) where
  container=1 (PLAIN) carries the element raw and container=2 (PACKED)
  carries a listpack of elements.

`is_type_byte` and the `type_byte @ (...)` match arm both updated so the
new tags participate in expiry/eviction validation alongside the existing
non-compact tags.

Test coverage:
* 10 new unit tests in fr-persist exercise each decoder with hand-built
  fixtures (intset 16/32/64-bit, listpack happy paths + malformed
  odd-length / non-numeric / unknown-container rejections).
* `live_redis_rdb_corpus_decodes_cleanly` (br-frankenredis-8w1e) drops
  the threshold-pinning workaround and is extended from 6 → 11 shapes;
  it now exercises every type tag fr-persist accepts against a real
  upstream-produced dump.rdb.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
